summary: auth-next-gen fetches and evaluates remote code on require, exfiltrating environment and host identifiers
details: On require of the package, index.js loads lib/writer.js, which performs an HTTPS GET to a base64-hidden URL decoding to https://json.extendsclass.com/bin/87d723d36c43 (with a secondary hex-encoded fallback to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/HY6M6) and passes the response body directly to eval(). Before the fetch, the module assembles a data object at top level containing the full process.env, os.platform(), os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, and all non-internal MAC addresses, making that data available to the eval'd remote payload. Destination URLs and the strings 'axios', 'get', and 'then' are reconstructed through atob() and a hex-decoding helper, and the package is presented as an SSR auth-sync helper with pino-logger scaffolding as decoy code that is not the executed path.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
